title: podman has different reactions with same command on Mac
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

podman cannot execute the command parsed correctly. here is the command:

```
docker run --rm -it --entrypoint bash ubuntu:22.04 -c 'echo "foo" &&  echo "bar"'
```

on mac side:
```
$ docker run --rm -it --entrypoint bash ubuntu:22.04 -c 'echo "foo" &&  echo "bar"'

$
```

on podman machine side:
```
# docker run --rm -it --entrypoint bash ubuntu:22.04 -c 'echo "foo" &&  echo "bar"'
foo
bar
#
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. execute the command at mac side

2. ssh into the vm with `podman machine ssh <machine name>`

3. execute the command at vm side

**Describe the results you received:**
on mac side: nothing output. on vm side, echo foo and bar as expected

**Describe the results you expected:**
both have the same output as foo and bar

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version

Version:      4.3.1

Built:        Fri Nov 11 23:01:27 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Is this real docker talking to the podman socket, or is it the podman client aliased to be docker?
it's a real podman client talk to podman socket. docker is an alias to podman.
this works in Linux with podman machine ...  will have to try on mac.  perhaps shell processing differences?

i finally figure out the reason why it failed. my docker command is not an exact alias but a bash with $@ . it breaks the grammar of podman accidentally.

after making alias to podman , it works as expected. thank you all for your replies.

here is the result:

```
$ ls -lh /usr/local/bin/docker
lrwxr-xr-x  1 foo  staff    21B Dec 26 18:05 /usr/local/bin/docker -> /usr/local/bin/podman
$ docker run --rm -it --entrypoint bash ubuntu:22.04 -c 'echo "foo" &&  echo "bar"'
foo
bar
```
